title: Shadow Droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:EvilSpaceThingies-DE2.png]] |name=Shadow Droid |designer= |manufacturer=[[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]] |line= |model=Imperial Shadow Droid{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planetintro|text=Byss and the Deep Core|int=Byss and the Deep Core}} |type=[[Droid starfighter/Legends|Droid starfighter]] |cost=Not available for sale |modifier= |sysmods= |length=6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=1,150 km/h |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=None |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=Equipped |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures=Stealth coating |armament=*[[Laser cannon/Legends|Laser cannons]] (2) *[[Ion cannon/Legends|Ion cannon]] (1) *Repeating [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] (1) *[[Electromagnetic pulse/Legends|Electromagnetic pulse]] gun (1) *[[Concussion missile/Legends|Concussion missile]] launcher (1) *[[Proton torpedo/Legends|Proton torpedo]] launcher (1) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=Disembodied organic brain (1)''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' |skeleton= |passengers=None |capacity=None |cargohandling= |consumables=None |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=[[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighter]] |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Dark Empire]] }} {{Quote|Something's not right. X-wings are always tenacious fighters. Why are they fleeing from the Shadow Droids?''"
"''They're frightened, sir… The Shadows are invincible!|Two unnamed Imperial officers during the [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Onderon]]|''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''}} '''Shadow Droids''' were [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by surgically implanted organic brains, making the Shadow [[Droid/Legends|Droid]] more of a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] rather than a true [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:ShadowDroid-SGtD.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Shadow Droids in action against [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing escort fighters]].]] When the brains became cybernetic hardware, they stripped the pilots of every remaining shred of Humanity, but augmented their reflexes and abilities far beyond anything they would have otherwise known. Emperor Palpatine had also given to his creations one final gift: the ability to touch the dark side, which made them essentially remote extensions of his own will. The Shadow Droid was a streamlined starfighter that was capable of keeping pace with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] in space, and in the atmosphere they utilized a maneuverable [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] system, but they could easily reach supersonic speeds with a short blast from their [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]]. These droids were also covered with a very glossy black alloy that made them difficult to pinpoint on [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scans. The droids' twin wings that were extended from the side of the control pad supported repeating [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and [[Electromagnetic pulse/Legends|electromagnetic pulse]] guns. The droids had two additional pylons to serve as racks and launchers for [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]]. The droids' two smaller dorsal lasers covered the rear field of fire, and a long-range antenna allowed the fighters to transmit their dense binary code stream between each other and their command ship. ==History== {{Quote|The imp's have some new kind of assault craft, sir… Makin' a mess of the SD's.|Unnamed officer to [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Hinch Beltane|Beltane]] during the [[Battle of Balmorra]]|}} The shadow droid project, under the guidance of Umak Leth, was one of the most twisted schemes embarked on by the Empire. Their biotic nature gave them additional advantages that automatons couldn't match; these sleek fighters were armed with the power of the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The Shadow Droids would never have existed if not for Emperor Palpatine's experimentations, and following the First Galactic Empire's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Palpatine's spirit inhabited a fresh [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and set in motion new plans of military conquest. The Emperor's throne world of Byss was the homeworld of the research for the Shadow Droids, and it soon spread to several think-tanks and manufacturing centers sequestered on hidden worlds throughout the Deep Core. The deadly machines were more cyborg than droid as they were implanted with the brains of mortally wounded [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] fighter ace [[TIE pilot|pilots]] and were able to manipulate the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Shadow Droids were first devised on the resurrected [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] throne [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. As manufacturing was secretly stepped up, the malevolent machines were produced on other [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] worlds. [[File:OperationShadowHandCoverArt-crop.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Shadow Droids fly behind [[X-1 Viper]]s.]] It is still very unclear how these droids were able to access and manipulate the dark side of the Force, but it is very likely they used it as a sixth sense for identifying potential threats. The Galactic Empire used the Shadow Droids during the [[Battle of Balmorra]] and in defense of Palpatine's flagship ''[[Eclipse II]]'' near [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. ==Appearances== *{{DoD|Jaws of the Sarlacc}} {{Mo}} *[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Shadow Droids}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *{{FactFile|23|MIL 1-4|Military Droids}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planetintro|text=Byss and the Deep Core|int=Byss and the Deep Core}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' {{C|Under ''Cyborgs''}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-12-tales-of-the-new-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 12: Tales of the New Republic}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schattendroide |es=Droide Sombra }} [[Category:Cyborgs]] [[Category:Droid starfighter classes]] [[Category:Imperial Department of Military Research products]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Dark Empire]]
